codeReview.summary: The cross-cmake-deps feature successfully verified that CMake target dependencies work correctly between ixland-system and ixland-libc-headers. The dependency graph shows proper linking: iox-core and iox-runtime both depend on ixland-libc-headers. The build order is correct with the interface library available before dependent targets. Include paths propagate correctly via target_link_libraries. No circular dependencies exist in the dependency graph.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The CMake configuration correctly uses target_link_libraries to propagate include paths. This pattern is consistent with modern CMake practices and ensures that consumers of iox-core and iox-runtime automatically get the ixland-libc-headers include paths.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: C_INCLUDES contains -I/Users/rudironsoni/src/github/rudironsoni/ixland/ixland-libc/include via target_link_libraries

sharedStateObservations.observation: The dependency graph (deps.dot) shows an acyclic DAG structure with ixland-libc-headers as a root node that both iox-runtime and iox-core depend on. This is the correct architecture for avoiding circular dependencies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff: deps.dot shows iox-runtime -> ixland-libc-headers, iox-core -> ixland-libc-headers, iox-core -> iox-runtime

sharedStateObservations.observation: CMake graphviz generation is available and useful for visualizing and verifying the dependency structure. The command cmake --graphviz=deps.dot generates a visual representation of the dependency graph.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows: cmake --graphviz=deps.dot was used to verify acyclic dependencies

summary: PASS - The cross-cmake-deps feature verified that CMake target dependencies work correctly. All observations confirm proper configuration: iox-core and iox-runtime link to ixland-libc-headers, include paths propagate correctly, no circular dependencies exist, and the full monorepo build succeeds. The feature is production-ready.
